description_long: This object is a streamlined, cylindrical bomb with a conical nose and a rounded, black-colored intake at the opposite end. The body is primarily a light gray color, featuring a series of small rivets or fasteners along the seams, suggesting a metallic construction. A single prominent stabilizing fin is mounted on the upper side, extending backward and slightly upward, which would aid in aerodynamic stability during flight. The front of the bomb has a turbine-like structure visible within the black intake, indicating it may be a jet-propelled or guided munition. The rear end tapers to a smaller diameter, with a central nozzle or exhaust port. The overall design is sleek and functional, with no visible external wiring, switches, or labels.
description: A streamlined, gray bomb with a black intake, visible turbine blades, and a single stabilizing fin. The body is metallic with riveted seams and a tapered rear nozzle.
description_view_0: Side view showing the conical body, black intake, and the single stabilizing fin extending upward.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the black intake and internal turbine blades, with the fin visible at the top.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the same conical body and fin, with the black intake and rivet details.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tapered nozzle and the fin mounted on top, with the intake not visible.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: No visible external detonation mechanism; detonation may be initiated internally or via remote signal, as there are no buttons, switches, or wires present.
